title: 🦞 OpenClaw: Adding Telegram & Slack Channels (WhatsApp & Discord Done)
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

OpenClaw Part 2: Adding Telegram & Slack Channels (WhatsApp & Discord Done)
Code & Cloud
9610 subscribers
2 likes
32 views
17 Feb 2026
Welcome back to the channel! In this comprehensive tutorial, I'll walk you through the entire process of connecting Telegram and Slack to OpenClaw, enabling you to control your computer remotely through these popular messaging platforms. Building on our previous videos where we set up OpenClaw on Windows and configured WhatsApp and Discord channels, today we're adding two more powerful communication channels to your automation toolkit.

What You'll Learn:

How to create and configure Telegram bots from scratch

Setting up Slack apps with proper permissions and scopes

Configuring Socket Mode and Event Subscriptions in Slack

Testing both channels with real-world tasks (file creation, browser automation)

Monitoring logs and chat history in OpenClaw

Controlling your computer from mobile devices

Why OpenClaw Matters: OpenClaw is a powerful open-source automation gateway that bridges the gap between your messaging apps and your computer. Think of it as your personal AI assistant that lives in your chat applications and can execute commands, manage files, browse the web, and perform complex tasks on your behalf. Whether you're a developer who wants to trigger builds from your phone or just someone who loves automating repetitive tasks, OpenClaw provides the infrastructure to make it happen.

Telegram Setup Overview: We start by downloading the Telegram Desktop client and creating a bot through BotFather. You'll learn how to get your access token, configure the OpenClaw JSON file, and pair your bot. We test everything with real commands – creating folders, generating text files, and even browser automation. The best part? Everything works seamlessly from the mobile app too, allowing true remote control from anywhere.

Slack Setup Overview: Slack integration requires more configuration but is equally powerful. We create a Slack app from scratch, enable Socket Mode, and carefully set up all required OAuth scopes including chat:write, channels:history, im:read, and more. Event subscriptions are configured to capture messages, and we install the app to your workspace. After adding both app token and bot token to OpenClaw, we test with browser automation and file management tasks.

Unified Command Center: One of OpenClaw's coolest features is unified message handling. All your conversations across Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, and Discord appear in the OpenClaw web interface. You can also explore detailed logs, filter by channel, and troubleshoot any issues that arise.


Timeline:

0:00 - Introduction and what we'll cover today
0:45 - Setting up Telegram desktop client
2:15 - Creating a Telegram bot and getting access token
4:30 - Configuring Telegram channel in OpenClaw
6:00 - Testing Telegram bot with file creation task
8:15 - Testing Telegram from mobile app
9:30 - Setting up Slack app from scratch
11:45 - Enabling Socket Mode and creating app token
13:30 - Configuring OAuth scopes for Slack bot
15:45 - Setting up Event Subscriptions
18:00 - Installing Slack app to workspace
19:30 - Connecting Slack channel in OpenClaw
21:15 - Testing Slack bot with browser automation

title: 🦞 Best Platform for Using OpenClaw Bot? (Telegram/Discord/Slack ...
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

From a developer’s perspective, which platform works best for interacting with the OpenClaw bot — **Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, or Slack**? Integrating the OpenClaw bot into your daily workflow can significantly enhance your productivity. When it comes to choosing the best platform for this integration, Redditors have shared their experiences and recommendations across various platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp. Here’s a succinct guide to help you make an informed decision:. "For dev workflows, Discord is honestly the best option if you need clean markdown and code blocks.". **Pros**: Reliable for work, threads keep contexts clean, good support for code blocks and tables. **Markdown/Code Output**: Discord and Slack excel in providing clean markdown and code block support. **Automation/Webhooks**: All platforms support webhooks, but Discord and Slack are often preferred for their ease of integration. By considering these insights from the Reddit community, you can choose the best platform for integrating your OpenClaw bot and optimize your workflow.

# Cron Jobs. # ​ Cron jobs (Gateway scheduler). openclaw cron run <job-id>. openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>`. ## ​ Tool-call equivalents (Gateway cron tool). ## ​ Where cron jobs are stored. ## ​ Concepts. ### ​ Jobs. ### ​ Schedules. #### ​ Main session jobs (system events). #### ​ Isolated jobs (dedicated cron sessions). ### ​ Payload shapes (what runs). #### ​ Webhook delivery flow. ### ​ cron.add params. ### ​ cron.update params. ### ​ cron.run and cron.remove params. `~/.openclaw/cron/runs/<jobId>.jsonl`. ## ​ Retry policy. ## ​ Configuration. store: "~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json",. ## ​ Maintenance. ### ​ Defaults. ### ​ How it works. openclaw cron add --name "Ops sweep" --cron "0 6 * * *" --session isolated --message "Check ops queue" --agent ops. openclaw cron edit <jobId> --agent ops. openclaw cron edit <jobId> --clear-agent`. openclaw cron run <jobId> --due`. `openclaw cron runs --id <jobId>`. `openclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --limit 50`. ## ​ Troubleshooting. ### ​ A recurring job keeps delaying after failures.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - Meta Intelligence
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout. Cron is the key feature that transforms OpenClaw from a "passive tool" into a "proactive assistant." Once configured, the agent automatically executes tasks and reports results at your specified times -- you don't need to remember what to check, don't need to open a terminal, and don't need to be at the computer.
